channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: What's next for Matt Gaetz: 5 possibilities
item.description: Here are five possibilities for what could come next for Gaetz following his announcement that he is taking his name out of the running to be attorney general in the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Gaetz withdrawal from AG nomination is among quickest in history
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) withdrew his nomination as President-elect Trump’s attorney general on Thursday, putting an end to a chaotic several days on Capitol Hill and making him one of the quickest in history to withdraw from Cabinet nominations.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KREX 5

item.title: No charges against California man arrested after vigilante teens set up sting
item.description: Authorities told NewsNation affiliate KTLA that deputies were tipped off that the suspect was allegedly meeting a minor at the park for “sexual purposes.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, NewsNation

item.title: What happened to JonBenét Ramsey? New documentary set to premiere this month
item.description: The death of JonBenét Ramsey remains unsolved and has been the subject of a number of documentaries and books.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Jarpe, NewsNation

item.title: Wisconsin man who faked his own kayaking death fled the country and won't come home, officials say
item.description: Borgwardt, 45, was reported missing on Aug. 12 after he failed to return home from kayaking on Big Green Lake, the Green Lake County Sheriff’s Office said at the time.
item.dc:creator: Ben Newhouse, WFLA, NBC

item.title: North Carolina official overseeing hurricane rebuilding efforts is no longer in role
item.description: The chief operating officer for the North Carolina Office of Recovery and Resiliency is no longer working in the position.
item.dc:creator: Makiya Seminera, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Illinois Supreme Court reverses actor Jussie Smollett's conviction
item.description: The Illinois Supreme Court has reversed the conviction of actor Jussie Smollett, who was found guilty of falsely reporting a 2019 hate crime, after he argued that the State's second prosecution was a due process violation.
item.dc:creator: Angelica Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Father of teen accused in Georgia school shooting pleads not guilty to charges against him
item.description: WINDER, Ga. (AP) — The father of a 14-year-old boy accused of carrying out a deadly mass shooting at Apalachee High School in Georgia pleaded not guilty on Thursday to the charges against him.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Assault allegations against Hegseth revealed in police report
item.description: A woman told police that she was sexually assaulted in 2017 by Pete Hegseth after he took her phone, blocked the door to a California hotel room and refused to let her leave, according to a detailed investigative report made public late Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: No charges filed against man arrested after teens set up sting
item.description: A 46-year-old California man ambushed by vigilante teens after he allegedly provided a girl with nude photos of himself and tried to arrange a sexual encounter with her is, so far, not facing charges.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KRON4

item.title: Florist gets death threats after refusing to serve Trump voters
item.description: The owner posted on social media that the business is now temporarily closed after receiving several death threats. She also made all of her social media accounts private.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Knox, NewsNation

item.title: Alabama to use nitrogen gas to execute man for 1994 murder of hitchhiker
item.description: An Alabama inmate convicted of the 1994 murder of a female hitchhiker is scheduled to be executed Thursday evening with nitrogen gas.
item.dc:creator: Kim Chandler, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Judge keeps death penalty a possibility for man charged in killings of 4 Idaho students
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — The death penalty will remain a possibility for a man charged with murder in the stabbing deaths of four University of Idaho students, a judge ruled Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ex-student pleads guilty to fatally shooting 3 University of Virginia football players in 2022
item.description: RICHMOND, Va. (AP) — A former University of Virginia student pleaded guilty Wednesday to fatally shooting three football players and wounding two other students on the campus in 2022. Christopher Darnell Jones Jr.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY and DENISE LAVOIE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New study shows voting for Native Americans is harder than ever
item.description: A study of access to polls during elections has found systemic barriers to voting on tribal lands contribute to substantial disparities in turnout.
item.dc:creator: Graham Lee Brewer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Susan Smith denied parole 30 years after drowning sons by rolling car into South Carolina lake
item.description: Susan Smith made national headlines in 1994 after she rolled her car into a lake with both of her children strapped in their seats.
item.dc:creator: Dustin George, KRON4

item.title: Florida man files lawsuit against Netflix over Tyson-Paul fight streaming issues
item.description: A class action lawsuit was filed against Netflix over alleged streaming issues during the fight between legendary brawler Mike Tyson and former YouTuber Jake Paul.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: Las Vegas man facing eviction tried to kill puppies, police say
item.description: A man allegedly tried to kill several puppies by covering them in cleaning products and placing them in a trash bag in a dumpster because he was getting evicted, according to police documents.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, Fox 5

item.title: Country Music Association Awards: who to watch
item.description: Post Malone and Morgan Wallen’s collaboration “I Had Some Help” could make both men big winners at Wednesday night's Country Music Association Awards.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON AP Entertainment Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Texas board advances plan to allow Bible material in elementary school lessons
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Texas' education board on Tuesday advanced a new Bible-infused curriculum that would be optional for schools to incorporate in kindergarten through fifth grades, one of the latest Republican-led efforts in the U.S. to incorporate more religious teaching into classrooms.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Almost 100 House Democrats push for Gaetz Ethics report’s release
item.description: Nearly 100 House Democrats wrote to the House Ethics Committee, urging them to publish its report on former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) and their investigation into his alleged misconduct after President-elect Trump nominated him to serve as attorney general.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, ABC 4

item.title: Mattel releases doll honoring EGOT winner Rita Moreno
item.description: Toy maker Mattel is releasing a doll honoring Emmy, Grammy, Oscar and Tony (EGOT) winner Rita Moreno, a prominent Latina actor.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WGN 9

item.title: Trump watches as Musk’s SpaceX launches Starship rocket
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump is expected to attend the launch of a SpaceX rocket in Texas on Tuesday, sources familiar with the plans confirmed, in the latest sign of Trump’s closeness to the company's billionaire CEO Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: California governor will not make clemency decision for Menendez brothers until new DA reviews case
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California Gov. Gavin Newsom said he won't make a clemency decision on the murder convictions of Erik and Lyle Menendez until newly elected Los Angeles County District Attorney Nathan Hochman reviews the nearly 35-year-old case.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: These are the best cruise lines for 2025
item.description: If you're looking to spend a vacation at sea but haven't decided which cruise line is best for your specific needs, a new online ranking shows the best options for every type of traveler, from families to luxury seekers. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Nvidia rivals focus on building a different kind of chip to power AI products
item.description: SANTA CLARA, Calif. (AP) — Building the current crop of artificial intelligence chatbots has relied on specialized computer chips pioneered by Nvidia, which dominates the market and made itself the poster child of the AI boom.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Speaker Johnson's 'epic' weekend with Trump shows strengths and limits of his power
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — There was House Speaker Mike Johnson walking behind President-elect Donald Trump's entourage into Saturday night's UFC fight at Madison Square Garden, his stature overcome by the enormity of the scene around him. And Johnson mugging with musicians Kid Rock and Jelly Roll.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump signals use of military for deportations
item.description: President-elect Trump recirculated a social media post Monday suggesting his incoming administration will declare an immigration national emergency and use military assets to support his mass deportation pledge, labeling the claim “TRUE!!!” On his Truth Social site, Trump at 4:03 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Beyoncé performing at halftime of NFL Christmas Day game on Netflix
item.description: Beyoncé is coming to your home on Christmas — provided you have Netflix and are tuning in to the Baltimore Ravens-Houston Texans game.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Spirit Airlines files for bankruptcy as financial losses pile up
item.description: Spirit Airlines said Monday that it has filed for bankruptcy protection and will attempt to reboot as it struggles to recover from the pandemic-caused swoon in travel and a failed attempt to sell the airline to JetBlue. Spirit, the biggest U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KOENIG, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Cruise line offers 4-year journey to 'skip forward' in wake of US presidential election
item.description: While the cruise line doesn't explicitly say the offer was brought on by the election, the two-year option was dubbed the 'Mid-Term Selection.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, Fox 5

item.title: Biden authorizes Ukraine to use US-supplied long-range missiles for deeper strikes inside Russia
item.description: President Joe Biden has authorized the use of U.S.-supplied long-range missiles by Ukraine to strike even deeper inside Russia, the latest easing of limitations meant to prevent the conflict from further spiraling, according to one U.S. official and three people familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Longest-waiting child in Arkansas foster care system finds forever family
item.description: After more than 6,100 days in the Arkansas foster care system, 17-year-old Jose has now found his forever family.
item.dc:creator: Mattison Gafner, WGN 9

item.title: Firehouse Subs pledges $25K donation after social media backlash from veteran
item.description: The company that owns Firehouse Subs has apologized after a Georgia veteran shared her family's negative interaction with a franchise owner on social media.
item.dc:creator: Christine Queally, WGN 9
